title: Leadership Lessons from Special Operations
excerpt: What years of Special Forces service taught me about leading teams in high-stakes environments, and how those principles apply to building a technology company.

children.text: Leadership in Special Operations is fundamentally different from what most people imagine. It is not about commanding from the front with bravado. It is about building trust, developing competence across your team, and making decisions under uncertainty with incomplete information.

During my time as an 18D (Special Forces Medical Sergeant), I learned that the most effective leaders are those who invest in their people. They create environments where every team member can operate independently while maintaining alignment with the mission.

When I founded Altivum Inc., I brought these same principles with me. Building a technology company is, in many ways, a mission. You need a clear objective, a capable team, and the ability to adapt when conditions change.

relatedPosts.title: Building AI Systems That Serve Veterans
relatedPosts.excerpt: How Altivum Inc. is leveraging artificial intelligence to create tools that help veterans.
